# MCPKit

> Swift library for integrating MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools into iOS/macOS apps. Host your app's functions as MCP tools for AI clients or connect to remote MCP servers.

MCPKit wraps the official MCP Swift SDK to simplify two use cases:
1. **Host MCP tools** - Register app functions as tools that AI clients (Claude Desktop, ChatGPT) can discover and call
2. **Connect to remote MCP servers** - Use 3rd party MCP tools (GitHub, Slack, Filesystem)

## Architecture

```
MCPManager (central orchestrator)
├── localServer: MCPLocalServer  ← Hosts tools for AI clients
├── connections: [MCPConnection] ← Remote MCP server connections
└── tools: [MCPTool]             ← All available tools
```

## Installation

```swift
dependencies: [
    .package(url: "https://github.com/user/MCPKit.git", from: "1.0.0")
]
```

**Targets:**
- `MCPKit` - Full bundle (Core + Tools)
- `Core` - MCP infrastructure only
- `Tools` - Predefined tools only
- `AnyLanguageModelBridge` - Integration with AnyLanguageModel (only use if your app uses AnyLanguageModel for LLM inference)

## Quick Start

```swift
import MCPKit

let manager = MCPManager()

// Start local server with tools
let local = try await manager.startLocalServer {
    ClipboardTool.tools
    CalendarTool.tools
}
try await manager.connect(local)

// Get tools for AI (prefixed names like mcp_local_clipboard_read)
let tools = manager.enabledTools

// Execute tool call from AI response
let result = try await manager.callTool("mcp_local_clipboard_read")
```

## Creating Custom Tools

### Simple tool (no arguments)
```swift
MCPTool(name: "ping", description: "Ping the app") { _ in
    return "pong"
}
```

### Tool with arguments
```swift
MCPTool(
    name: "play_music",
    description: "Play a song",
    inputSchema: .object([
        "type": .string("object"),
        "properties": .object([
            "song": .object(["type": .string("string")])
        ]),
        "required": .array([.string("song")])
    ])
) { args in
    let song = args?["song"]?.stringValue ?? "Unknown"
    return "Now playing: \(song)"
}
```

### Throwing tool
```swift
MCPTool(
    name: "fetch_data",
    description: "Fetch data from API",
    throwingHandler: { args in
        return try await api.fetchData()
    }
)
```

## AnyLanguageModel Integration

Only use if your app uses AnyLanguageModel for LLM inference:

```swift
import AnyLanguageModelBridge

// MCPTool conforms to AnyLanguageModel.Tool
let session = LanguageModelSession(
    model: AnthropicLanguageModel(apiKey: key),
    tools: manager.tools
)
```

## Predefined Tools

| Tool | Operations |
|------|------------|
| ClipboardTool | clipboard_read, clipboard_write |
| NotificationTool | notification_schedule, notification_request_permission |
| URLOpenerTool | url_open |
| CalendarTool | calendar_list_events, calendar_create_event |

## Key Types

- `MCPManager`: Central manager for local tools and remote connections
- `MCPTool`: Struct wrapping MCP.Tool with execute handler
- `MCPLocalServer`: Hosts your tools for external AI clients
- `MCPConnection`: Client connection to remote MCP servers
- `MCPToolProvider`: Protocol for grouping related tools
- `MCPToolsBuilder`: Result builder for declarative tool registration

## Tool Naming Convention

Tools are prefixed when accessed via MCPManager:
- Server-side: `clipboard_read`
- Client-side: `mcp_local_clipboard_read` (format: `mcp_{server}_{toolName}`)

## Requirements

- iOS 17.0+ / macOS 14.0+
- Swift 6.1+

## Core Sources

- [MCPManager.swift](Sources/Core/MCPManager.swift): Central orchestrator for MCP functionality
- [MCPTool.swift](Sources/Core/Models/MCPTool.swift): Tool definition with execute handler
- [MCPLocalServer.swift](Sources/Core/MCPLocalServer.swift): Hosts tools for AI clients
- [MCPConnection.swift](Sources/Core/MCPConnection.swift): Remote MCP server connection
- [MCPToolProvider.swift](Sources/Core/Protocols/MCPToolProvider.swift): Protocol for tool providers
- [MCPToolsBuilder.swift](Sources/Core/Protocols/MCPToolsBuilder.swift): Result builder for tools

## Predefined Tool Sources

- [ClipboardTool.swift](Sources/Tools/ClipboardTool.swift): System clipboard operations
- [CalendarTool.swift](Sources/Tools/CalendarTool.swift): Calendar event operations
- [NotificationTool.swift](Sources/Tools/NotificationTool.swift): Local notification operations
- [URLOpenerTool.swift](Sources/Tools/URLOpenerTool.swift): URL opening operations

## AnyLanguageModel Bridge Sources

- [MCPTool+Tool.swift](Sources/AnyLanguageModelBridge/MCPTool+Tool.swift): MCPTool conformance to AnyLanguageModel.Tool
- [SchemaConverter.swift](Sources/AnyLanguageModelBridge/SchemaConverter.swift): MCP schema to GenerationSchema conversion
- [ValueConverter.swift](Sources/AnyLanguageModelBridge/ValueConverter.swift): Value type conversion between MCP and AnyLanguageModel

## Optional

- [Tools.swift](Sources/Tools/Tools.swift): Convenience accessor for all predefined tools
